headline: ChemNet v2 (10-node lineup+pitcher graph, residual objective) crosses none of the four predictive gates. Pitcher node delivered measurable signal (Pearson r 0.089 -> 0.155, RMSE 4.59 -> 4.53) but neither change closed the gap to the >= 0.30 / <= 4.0 thresholds, and the GNN remains marginally worse than its non-graph MLP baseline. Synergy score (gnn - baseline) reverses sign on the residual prediction (-0.037 vs +0.026 in v1), confirming the synergy framing is not recoverable on per-game wOBA. Recommendation: retire ChemNet from flagship-candidate list.

architecture_changes.graph_topology: 9 lineup nodes -> 10 nodes (lineup + opposing starter, star overlay on band)
architecture_changes.node_features_pitcher: [rolling_FIP_proxy, K%, BB%, HR_per_9, GB%] z-normalised within cohort
architecture_changes.objective: residual prediction (actual_total_woba - sum_of_parts) replacing absolute target
architecture_changes.sum_of_parts_baseline: fixed slot-PA schedule [4.6, 4.5, 4.4, 4.3, 4.2, 4.1, 4.0, 3.9, 3.8] times slot rolling wOBA
architecture_changes.absolute_equivalent_predictions: pred_absolute = sum_of_parts + pred_residual (for both GNN and baseline MLP)

training_stats.epochs_note: Training was killed after 1 epoch on the second pass (eager-save checkpoint at best val_gnn=21.4253) to preserve wall-clock budget for validation. A 9-epoch run completed in a previous attempt and showed val_gnn flat at 21.41-21.47 across epochs, confirming the architecture saturates fast and additional epochs do not change the gate verdict.

residual_only_metrics.note: Same numerical predictions evaluated against the residual target (actual - sum_of_parts) instead of the absolute target. Useful for diagnosing whether the residual objective recovered any signal that the absolute view obscures.
residual_only_metrics.interpretation: sum_of_parts on its own correlates r=0.10 with actual_total_woba; adding the GNN/baseline residual prediction lifts this to r~0.15, but the GNN does not outperform the MLP on the residual scale either.

caveats: Training was truncated to 1 epoch on the saved checkpoint (eager-save). A separate 9-epoch run on the same architecture showed val_gnn never improved beyond ~21.41 MSE (RMSE ~4.63 on residual scale); the gate verdict would not change with more epochs.

The residual scale variance is essentially the same as the absolute scale variance (sum_of_parts is a weak baseline because rolling slot wOBAs vary by only +/- 0.04 on the scale of total game wOBA standard deviation 4.75). The residual objective therefore did not simplify the prediction problem materially.
GPU was contended with 4 concurrent agents; training-epoch wall time inflated from ~1 s/epoch on smoke (160 graphs) to ~130 s/epoch on the full 31,453-graph cohort.
v1 checkpoint preserved (37 KB at models/chemnet_v1.pt) and unchanged.
